tasks.text: Optimize rayAABB_inv: This is 29.4% exclusive. The current implementation likely uses divisions. Replace with precomputed inv_dir and avoid division in hot path.
Inline IntersectBVH: Move implementation to object/object.h as static inline to allow compiler optimization across function boundary
Optimize rayTriangle: The Möller-Trumbore algorithm has a division (1.0f/a) per triangle test. Look for opportunities to reduce operations or use faster approximations.
Inline IntersectBVH: Move implementation to object/object.h as static inline to allow compiler optimization across function boundary
Re-apply float3 by-value to rayAABB_inv callers and rebuild without stale PGO data
Optimize rayTriangle: Restructure Möller-Trumbore to reduce dependency chains and improve pipelining
Optimize BVH traversal: Reduce memory accesses in the inner loop by loading AABB data into local variables

notes.text: Baseline: avg 9.14ms, 1090 frames in 10s. Top hotspot is rayAABB_inv at 29.4% exclusive time, IntersectBVH at 43.4% inclusive. This is the BVH traversal and AABB intersection testing.

Key insight: IntersectBVH is called per-object per-ray. The world AABB test in RayTraceRowFunc already culls objects, but IntersectBVH is still called for objects that pass. IntersectBVH transforms the ray to local space (18 mults, 6 adds), computes invDir/bias (3 divs, 3 mults), then traverses the BVH. Making IntersectBVH inline could help the compiler optimize across the function boundary.

rayAABB_inv float3 by-value change: avg 9.06ms (was 9.14ms), +0.9% improvement. image_mse=0.00 (visually identical). The change passes float3 by value instead of const float* pointers, allowing better register allocation.

Inlining rayAABB_inv directly in IntersectBVH caused -16% regression. Likely due to increased code complexity preventing compiler optimizations or increased register pressure. Restored to baseline + the float3 by-value change (which gave +0.9%).

Regression after re-applying float3 by-value: avg 9.806ms vs 9.14ms baseline. This may be due to stale profile-guided optimization data (.profdata). Need to rebuild without PGO or with fresh profile data. image_mse=0.00, visually identical.
Re-applying float3 by-value change to rayAABB_inv. Previous regression likely due to stale .profdata PGO data. Will delete .profdata and rebuild clean.
float3 by-value change to rayAABB_inv caused consistent -7-10% regression. Restoring. The change likely increased register pressure or hurt instruction scheduling. Need a different approach.
